message:
🌱 Add dapr: Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing mission (#1677)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "dapr-2308-enhance-pub-sub-cloud-event-handling-to-support-existing-messaging-inf",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "dapr: Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing messaging infrastructure",
+    "description": "Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing messaging infrastructure. Requested by 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current dapr deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review dapr configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
Currently, Dapr is overly opinionated with regard to enforcing the publishing of messages conforming to the Cloud Event specification."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing…",
+        "description": "IMHO, when interfacing two completely different systems, you're always going to have some sort of boundary where you have to convert from one data-format to another.

In this case, you could have a translation service that receives publishes from Dapr destined for the other service, terminating the
```yaml
{
    "customerName": "Bruce Wayne",
    "loyaltyId": "1",
    "orderTotal": 35.91,
    "orderItems": [
        {
            "menuItemId": 3,
            "menuItemName": "Latte",
            "quantity": 5
        },
        {
            "menuItemId": 1,
            "menuItemName": "Americano",
            "quantity": 2
        },
        {
            "quantity": 2,
            "menuItemId": 2,
            "menuItemName": "Caramel Macchiato"
        }
    ],
    "orderId": "294111f8-0551-48a3-a48f-c3363c4116ad",
    "orderDate": "2020-10-07T19:42:27.513+0000",
    "storeId": "Redmond"
}
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade dapr to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade dapr:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr
helm list -n dapr
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Enhance pub/sub cloud event handling to support existing…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "IMHO, when interfacing two completely different systems, you're always going to have some sort of boundary where you have to convert from one data-format to another.

In this case, you could have a translation service that receives publishes from Dapr destined for the other service, terminating the tracing span, translating the event to what the other system recognizes, and publishing it.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{
    "customerName": "Bruce Wayne",
    "loyaltyId": "1",
    "orderTotal": 35.91,
    "orderItems": [
        {

        }
    ],

    "orderDate": "2020-10-07T19:42:27.513+0000",
    "storeId": "Redmond"
}",

+      ]
